100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On June 18, 1926, the U.S. Senate was poised to debate the proposed Child Labor Amendment, aiming to grant Congress the power to regulate child labor, a contentious issue in American society. Meanwhile, in the United Kingdom, Prime Minister Stanley Baldwin faced mounting pressure over the government's handling of the General Strike, with labor tensions still simmering. On the international front, tensions in the Middle East persisted as British forces clashed with Arab rebels in Palestine, highlighting the region's ongoing unrest.
Updated: Thursday, June 18, 2026 at 09:35:22 AM CDT
